source_or_gap: Pinned local BugBrain project snapshot and unresolved cross-project/external support for hardware-explicit cognition, signed context, action governance, and durable semantic memory.
chapter_action_policy: Update existing chapters with bounded implementation-reference and negative-case mechanisms after cross-project deduplication. Retain Context Horizon and graph compilation as additional evidence for the provisional Durable Semantic Memory and Knowledge Lattices chapter boundary.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge hardware ownership, context, approval, security, supply-chain, resource, benchmark, and readiness lessons into existing owners. Re-evaluate durable-memory material jointly with BeastBrain, Trainer, Best Model, and independent sources.
boundary_decision_rationale: BugBrain supplies a strong hardware-explicit and integrated Context Horizon implementation, but its primary concepts map to existing chapters and do not independently justify a new chapter. It strengthens rather than resolves the BeastBrain-derived durable-memory boundary.
external_literature_need: embedded and real-time AI runtime architecture primary sources
long-term agent memory, signed context, and crash-consistent storage primary sources
capability security, approval binding, and tamper-evident audit primary sources
hardware roots of trust, entropy, secure boot, and key-rotation primary sources
active inference and world-model validation primary sources
integrated information theory measurement and estimator-validity primary sources
continual learning, EWC, STDP, and consolidation primary sources
proof_or_test_backlog: trace BugBrain mechanisms at symbol level into CCA, MoECOT, Trainer, and Best Model
restore bridge dependencies and run bridge default and Bluetooth suites in a pinned environment
locate or regenerate a structured Pi HIL report with a cognition-relevant workload
generate storage-budget equations from graph constants and test a bounded full-format artifact
add property/fuzz tests for ContextHandle bounds, Context Horizon restore/revocation, intent binding, and security framing
compare active-inference rankings with and without the epistemic term on fixed candidates
require parameter/checkpoint changes before evaluating world-model, sleep, or self-modification claims
evidence_transition_precondition: exact source surfaces mapped to each book claim
independent external sources ingested for scientific and security claims
hardware and numeric results reproduced or retained as source-reported only
accepted Appendix C transition recorded before any support promotion
required_before_drafting: mine Corben's Trainer, Corben's Best Model Possible, and any newly extracted projects
keep raw private project material outside the public repository
separate design, interface presence, runtime wiring, learned effect, host test, emulator result, and hardware result
preserve clippy/readiness contradictions, budget arithmetic, security-root caveats, and standalone-module boundaries
promotion_blockers: bridge and target-hardware suites were not reproduced
no retained complete trained-manifest set or Pi performance report was established
several named cognitive modules are standalone, random, simplified, or not effect-validated
security protocol structure does not establish secret custody or hardware root of trust
cross-project and independent-literature deduplication remain incomplete
source-publication permission is not recorded for the raw tree
residuals: Pi HIL and trained artifact lineage remains unresolved
old readiness reports lack sufficient gate-policy identity
bridge dependency restoration and interop testing remain
later-project repairs of world-model, security-root, learning, and audit gaps are unknown
next_action: Mine Corben's Trainer next, then Corben's Best Model Possible, before the cross-project provenance and new-chapter decision pass.
non_claims: does not claim BugBrain capability, safety, security, consciousness, efficiency, production readiness, AGI, or ASI
does not reproduce bridge, Pi, HIL, OTA, vision, audio, swarm, GPU, trained-model, or deployed results
does not treat the 250-million-neuron constant as a trained artifact
does not add the proposed durable-memory chapter
does not promote an ASI Stack support state
